DECLARATION OF JAMES COY DRISCOLL IN OPPOS TO X-DEFT CAZAHOUS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT MARUYAMA, YUKIKO MARUYAMA, KIMIKO

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: LINDA YEN, ESQ. FOR CROSS DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, PHILIP CAZAHOUS. JAMES COY DRISCOLL, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. CROSS-DEFENDANT PHILIP CAZAHOUS'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE OBJECTIONS WERE MADE WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,350, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MASTER CALENDAR MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-13-2004 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF PHILIP J. CAZAHOUS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 1/10/05 IS CONTINUED TO 7/11/05 @ 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. THE CURRENT MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WILL BE VACATED. A NEW SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WILL BE SET AND MAILED BY THE COURT. DISCOVERY ACCORDING TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE JOHN MUNTER; CLERKS: DIANNE BOFFI/RANI EDWARDS; REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA (206)
ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: JAMES COY DRISCOLL, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, YUKIKO MARUYAMA AND KIMIKO MARUYAMA. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA IS GRANTED AS THE SUBPOENA IS OVERLY BROAD AND INVADES DEFENDANTS' AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' RIGHT TO PRIVACY WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE. CROSS-DEFENDANT MUST DETERMINE THE PHYSICAL HARM THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AND LIMIT ANY REQUEST FOR MEDICAL RECORDS TO TREATMENT FOR THAT ALLEGED PHYSICAL HARM. AS THE SUBPOENA SEEKS ALL MEDICAL RECORDS RELATED TO THE CARE AND SUPPORT OF THE DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS, WITHOUT ANY LIMITATION, IT IS OVERLY BROAD. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
